title: n8n node expression syntax error
symptom: A node fails with 'Invalid expression' or 'Expression evaluation error' when referencing data from previous nodes.
not_this_if: Error is about missing node (node was deleted)
Expression uses valid JS but n8n doesn't support it
root_cause: n8n expressions use a specific syntax: {{ $json.fieldName }} for the current item, {{ $node['NodeName'].json.fieldName }} for other nodes. Common mistakes include using dot notation on the $node shorthand, forgetting quotes around node names with spaces, or using JavaScript methods not available in the expression sandbox.
fix_steps: Check expression syntax: use {{ $json.fieldName }} for current node data
Avoid JS methods like .map() or .filter() in simple expressions — use a Code node instead
Test expressions in the expression editor (click the expression toggle on the field)
agent_instruction: Fix the n8n expression syntax. Use {{ $json.fieldName }} for current node, {{ $node['Node Name'].json.fieldName }} for other nodes. Complex JS logic should be in a Code node, not in field expressions.
version_notes: Expression syntax is stable across n8n versions
